text: Here we're inside the right pleural cavity, the right side of the chest of the patient, and you see that we're going between ribs with an electrocautery device, which burns the tissue.
Once the hole has been created, the trocar will now be coming into view.
This trocar is then attached to the robotic surgical platform.
Here you see instruments being introduced.
This is a grasper, which we're going to use to grasp objects.
And we're moving the lung out of the way.
And you can see the right phrenic nerve, which must be spared.
Here the attachments of the pericardial cysts to the fat is being divided.
You can notice the beating heart underneath the sac.
These attachments to the pericardial cysts are divided with an electrocardiogram device on the robot as well.
These attachments must be carefully divided in a meticulous fashion so you don't enter the pericardial sac of the heart.
The final attachments to the pericardial cyst are here being released.
And here is the completely freed pericardial cyst.
Now you're going to see a endobag being placed through one of the trocarcytes and the pericardial cyst is going to be placed into this bag.
And this bag is then removed with the cyst specimen, which appears in this image.
